title: Audio playback failures are unhandled
reasoning: HTMLMediaElement.play() returns a Promise that can reject when audio playback is blocked, the asset cannot load, or the browser refuses playback. The rejection is ignored, producing an unhandled promise rejection in affected browsers while the UI continues updating. This is noisy in production and can fail tests or error monitoring if unhandled rejections are treated as failures.
reproduction: Mock Audio.prototype.play to return Promise.reject(new Error('blocked')), click a light, and observe an unhandled rejection.
recommendation: Catch and intentionally ignore or log playback failures, for example by storing the play promise and attaching .catch(() => undefined) while keeping the state update independent.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included, and there is no coverage for rejected audio playback promises.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test that mocks Audio.prototype.play to reject, clicks a light, asserts the message still changes, and verifies no unhandled rejection escapes.
minimumFixScope: Update handleLightClick in packages/teacher/src/features/widgets/trafficLight/trafficLight.tsx and add a focused test for rejected playback.
